revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]Created page with '<!--- Don't mess with this line! --->{{New unreviewed article|date={{subst:CURRENTMONTHNAME}} {{subst:CURRENTYEAR}}}} <!--- Write your article below this line --...'
revision.text:
<!--- Don't mess with this line! --->{{New unreviewed article|date=December 2014}} <!--- Write your article below this line ---> The Religious Organizations Law (宗教団体法 Shūkyō Dantai Hō?) was passed by the [[Japanese Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for ...By Christopher Ives Page 39</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism By Jørn Borup Page 29</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was abrogated on December 28 1945, leading to the passing of the "Religious Corporations Ordinance.<ref>Religion in Japanese History By Joseph Mitsuo Kitagawa Page 271</ref>
==References== {{reflist}} <!--- After listing your sources please cite them using inline citations and place them after the information they cite. Please see http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:REFB for instructions on how to add citations. ---> * * * *
<!--- STOP! Be warned that by using this process instead of Articles for Creation, this article is subject to scrutiny. As an article in "mainspace", it will be DELETED if there are problems, not just declined. If you wish to use AfC, please return to the Wizard and continue from there. --->

revision.comment: Added {{[[Template:citation style|citation style]]}} and {{[[Template:orphan|orphan]]}} tags to article ([[WP:TW|TW]])
reword to avoid disambiguation page and tag as requiring ISBNs for citations

deleted list of see also. The Peace Preservation Law and the Religious Organizations Law are completely different. The Peace Preservation Law was used to deal with dissidents, while the Religious Organizations Law was used to regulate religions
citation style fixed
reference fix
reference fix
Undid revision 639460903 by [[Special:Contributions/Greg723|Greg723]] ([[User talk:Greg723|talk]]) restore valid "See also" entry (they don't have to be "the same")
fix citation format re names
/* References */clean up using [[Project:AWB|AWB]] (10809)
clean up / template rename per request to avoid confusion using [[Project:AWB|AWB]] (10812)
/* top */[[WP:AWB/GF|General fixes]], removed orphan tag using [[Project:AWB|AWB]]
revision.text:
{{Lacking ISBN|date=December 2014}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book | title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |first = Christopher |last = Ives | year=2009 | page=39 | isbn=}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book | title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=Brill Academic Pub |first = Jørn |last = Borup| year=2008| page=29| isbn=}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book | title=Religion in Japanese History |publisher=Columbia University Press |first = Joseph Mitsuo |last = Kitagawa | year=1966 | page=271| isbn=}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]]
==References== {{reflist}}
[[Category:Legal history of Japan]]

{{Lacking ISBN|date=December 2014}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book | title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |first = Christopher |last = Ives | year=2009 | page=39 | isbn=}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book | title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=Brill Academic Pub |first = Jørn |last = Borup| year=2008| page=29| isbn=}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book | title=Religion in Japanese History |publisher=Columbia University Press |first = Joseph Mitsuo |last = Kitagawa | year=1966 | page=271| isbn=}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

revision.comment: Added publisher location and ISBNs; resolved maintenance issue so banner removed
revision.text:
The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations" ([[Wikipedia:Shortdesc helper|Shortdesc helper]])
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.ph/0rz9J</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.ph/0rz9J</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}}{{State Shinto}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

revision.comment: Tagging pages with bare URLs for citation, prior to follow up with tools (via [[WP:JWB]])
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.ph/0rz9J {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}}{{State Shinto}} [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:1939 in religion]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}}{{State Shinto}} [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1939 in Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1939 in law]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:1939 in religion]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]] [[en:Religious Organizations Law]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]

{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[World War II]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{State Shinto}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1519018]] Wikidata name: Religious Organizations Law [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the [[:en:National Diet|National Diet]] in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]], the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *[[:en:Peace Preservation Law|Peace Preservation Law]] *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *[[:en:Institute of Divinities|Institute of Divinities]] *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{State Shinto}}

religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1519018]] Wikidata name: Religious Organizations Law [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:56, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the {{ill|National Diet|en|National Diet|WD=Q202384|lt=National Diet}} in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}, the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|Peace Preservation Law|en|Peace Preservation Law|WD=Q657216|lt=Peace Preservation Law}} *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *{{ill|Institute of Divinities|en|Institute of Divinities|WD=Q11590709|lt=Institute of Divinities}} *[[Bureau of Religions]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{State Shinto}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gesetz über die Religionsgemeinschaften]][[en:Religious Organizations Law]][[es:Ley sobre Comunidades Religiosas]][[fr:Loi sur les organisations religieuses]][[ja:宗教団体法]][[zh:宗教團體法]]
{{Short description|Japanese law passed in 1939 that gave authority over religious organizations}} The {{Nihongo|'''Religious Organizations Law'''|宗教団体法|Shūkyō Dantai Hō}} was a Japanese law passed by the {{ill|National Diet|en|National Diet|WD=Q202384|lt=National Diet|1=National Diet}} in 1939 and enacted in 1940.<ref>{{cite book|last=Ives|first=Christopher|url=https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives|title=Imperial-Way Zen: Ichikawa Hakugen's Critique and Lingering Questions for Buddhist Ethics|publisher=University of Hawaii Press|year=2009|isbn=978-0-8248-3331-2|location=Honolulu|page=[https://archive.org/details/imperialwayzenic00ives/page/n49 39]|url-access=limited}}</ref><ref>https://archive.today/20230316011119/https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=8863 {{bare URL inline|date=April 2023}}</ref> The law gave the state authority control over religious organizations.<ref>{{cite book|last=Borup|first=Jørn|url=https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru|title=Japanese Rinzai Zen Buddhism|publisher=[[Brill Publishers|Brill]]|year=2008|isbn=978-90-04-16557-1|location=Leiden|page=[https://archive.org/details/japaneserinzaize00boru/page/n41 29]|url-access=limited}}</ref> Following Japan's defeat in {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the Religious Organizations Law was repealed on December 28, 1945, and replaced by the "Religious Corporations Ordinance".<ref>{{cite book|last=Kitagawa|first=Joseph Mitsuo|url=https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita|title=Religion in Japanese History|publisher=Columbia University Press|year=1966|isbn=978-0-231-02834-9|location=New York|page=[https://archive.org/details/religioninjapane0000kita/page/271 271]|url-access=registration}}</ref>
==See also== *{{ill|Peace Preservation Law|en|Peace Preservation Law|WD=Q657216|lt=Peace Preservation Law|1=Peace Preservation Law}} *[[Secular Shrine Theory]] *{{ill|Institute of Divinities|en|Institute of Divinities|WD=Q11590709|lt=Institute of Divinities|1=Institute of Divinities}} *[[Bureau of Religions]]
